svalbard-northern-lights-dog-sledding.name: Northern Lights Dog Sledding
svalbard-northern-lights-dog-sledding.description: Dog sledding under the aurora at 78°N — the most extreme aurora experience on earth. A team of Alaskan huskies pulls you across the frozen Adventdalen valley while the sky shifts green and violet overhead. Peak season runs November through February, when the polar night guarantees total darkness and KP3+ activity is frequent.

svalbard-polar-bear-safari-snowmobile.name: Polar Bear Safari by Snowmobile
svalbard-polar-bear-safari-snowmobile.description: Multi-hour snowmobile expedition into Svalbard's eastern wilderness, where the polar bear population outnumbers humans 3:1. Your licensed guide carries a rifle — mandatory outside Longyearbyen settlement limits. Routes vary by ice and weather conditions, covering 60–120 km across glacial valleys and frozen fjords.

svalbard-glacier-hiking-expedition.name: Glacier Hiking Expedition
svalbard-glacier-hiking-expedition.description: Svalbard has 60% glacier coverage. Guided day hikes on Longyearbreen and Nordenskiöldbreen with crampons and ice axes provided. You walk across blue ice, through meltwater channels, and past crevasse fields that have been forming since the last ice age. The guide reads the ice conditions live — no two trips follow the same route.

svalbard-snowmobile-russian-ghost-town.name: Snowmobile to Pyramiden Ghost Town
svalbard-snowmobile-russian-ghost-town.description: A full-day snowmobile expedition across Tempelfjorden to the abandoned Soviet mining settlement of Pyramiden. The town was evacuated in 1998 and left frozen in time — Lenin's northernmost bust still faces the glacier. The 200 km round trip crosses open fjord ice and glacial moraines. Your guide carries a rifle for polar bear territory.

svalbard-sail-and-hike-expedition.name: Sail & Hike the Svalbard Archipelago
svalbard-sail-and-hike-expedition.description: A multi-day sailing expedition through the Svalbard archipelago with daily shore hikes on uninhabited islands. You sleep aboard a traditional sailing vessel, navigate between glaciers and bird cliffs, and hike terrain that has no trails — only coordinates. 57hours runs these with professional mountain guides and experienced skippers.

svalbard-arctic-wildlife-photography.name: Arctic Wildlife Photography Tour
svalbard-arctic-wildlife-photography.description: Dedicated photography expedition targeting Svalbard's Arctic wildlife: polar bears, Arctic foxes, walrus colonies, and seabird cliffs with thousands of breeding Brünnich's guillemots. Led by a professional wildlife photographer and an armed guide. You travel by zodiac boat along the coastline, with long stops at known wildlife sites. Bring a 200–500mm lens.

svalbard-northern-lights-photography-cha.name: Northern Lights Photography Chase
svalbard-northern-lights-photography-cha.description: A specialist aurora photography tour at 78°N during Solar Maximum 25. Your guide monitors the KP index and cloud cover in real time, driving to the clearest skies within a 30 km radius of Longyearbyen. At KP3+ with clear sky, the aurora at this latitude fills the entire sky — not just the northern horizon. The guide helps with camera settings and long-exposure technique.

svalbard-midnight-sun-kayaking.name: Midnight Sun Fjord Kayaking
svalbard-midnight-sun-kayaking.description: Paddle through Adventfjorden and Isfjorden under 24-hour daylight. The water is calm, the glaciers calve into the fjord ahead of you, and the only sound is your paddle. Suitable for beginners — the guide provides full instruction and dry suits. You may spot ringed seals, bearded seals, and beluga whales from water level.
